rows.title: RWBY Cyoa
rows.titleText:
The World of Remnant is one where an advanced civilization is constantly under threat from shadowy monsters called Grimm, confining people to mainly living in walled off cities. Despite this, the world is oddly hopeful. The Grimm are attracted to negative emotions and are each strong enough to kill any untrained person with minimal effort, yet elite warriors called Huntsmen and Huntresses cut them down with ease using their high-tech weapons and fantastical abilities. The Brother's, the gods of light and darkness respectively, are worshipped in Remnant, despite being absent gods.

Unbeknown to the vast majority of Remnant, two cursed, feuding immortals, Ozma and Salem, are respectively trying to maintain and end civilization. Their constant conflict has reached a stalemate once again, and The Brothers, sick of the two's perceived stagnation, have chosen to send in you to shake things up.

objects.title: Origin
objects.text: What's your story?

objects.title: Body
objects.text: Age, Sex, etc.

objects.title: Race
objects.text: Human or Faunus?

objects.title: Reincarnation
objects.text:
You're either reborn in a new body or take over a pre-made body for you at whatever point in time you wish. Do not worry, the body itself was soulless and running on auto-pilot as it waited for you to insert.

You can decide how many memories you keep and what their life was like, such as living in a normal middle class home or being a rich heir to a corporation. Your powers may factor into how your body lived.

objects.title: Drop-In
objects.text: You have no history in the world you're going. You can still choose a new appearance from your old one but you don't gain anything besides the things you get here.

objects.title: Orphan
objects.text: Your story is an unfortunate one, isn't it? You have no family, so you could likely vanish without much fanfare. For what it's worth, this is the origin of many great heroes.

objects.title: Civilian
objects.text: You're simply one of many people that huntsmen and huntresses save from Grimm. Though you'll normally be safe, you aren't ready to fight, if you'll ever need to.

objects.title: Huntsman
objects.text: If you're not already a huntsman, then you're actively training to be one, either as an apprentice to a current one or as a student at an academy in a four-member team.

objects.title: Wealthy
objects.text: While you're certainly no Schnee, your family has a fair bit of wealth, such as from owning a successful business or two.

objects.title: Schnee Family
objects.text: You're now a member of the Schnee family, making you arguably an enemy of the White Fang and probably at least a little racist. You're also very wealthy, due to the Schnee Dust Company being Remnant's biggest corporation.

objects.title: Inner Circle
objects.text: For whatever reason you either already are or soon will be in the inner circle of Ozpin or Salem, depending on your personality and whether you'd rather preserve or completely destroy civilization.

rows.title: Reincarnation
rows.titleText: Choose your new life. This choice will determine many things after you start your journey, in addition to also making small changes in the cyoa, such as discounts.

objects.title: Young
objects.text: You are under 13 years old.

objects.title: Teen
objects.text: You are 13-19 years old.

objects.title: Adult
objects.text: You are 20-40 years old.

objects.title: Older
objects.text: You are 41-60 years old.

objects.title: Elder
objects.text: You are above 60 years old.

objects.title: Short
objects.text: You're short for your age and gender, over 10cm (4in) below the average, in fact.

objects.title: Average
objects.text: You're within 10cm (4in) of the average for your age and gender, either above or below.

objects.title: Tall
objects.text: You're tall for your age and gender, over 10cm (4in) above the average, in fact.

objects.title: Human
objects.text: You know 'em, you probably are one, humans are the 'baseline' in this world. They can use Aura, they don't get discriminated upon because they run the world, and they can have all kinds of wacky hair colors in this world.

objects.title: Faunus
objects.text: Although they're just humans with one extra biological feature and enhanced senses, they get discriminated upon because humans like racism. As a baseline, they have the one animal trait, which is normally ears, a tail, horns, claws, or visibly non-human eyes.

rows.title: Race
rows.titleText: This world's two humanoid races are Humans and Faunus. Humans are boring, but Faunus, who are individually just a human with a trait corresponding to a random animal, have extra stuff with no clear downside. When a human and a Faunus have a child, the child will always be a Faunus, namely one with a trait from the same animal as their Faunus parent, such as someone with cat ears having a child with a cat tail, retractable cat claws, or maybe just cat ears. When two faunus have a child, assuming their respective animal traits are from different animals, their child will manifest a trait from the animal corresponding to one random parent.

objects.title: Sensory
objects.text: This is where you'd get your animal ears for enhanced hearing or non-human eyes for vision.

objects.title: Mobility
objects.text: This is where you get something like wings for gliding (probably too weak to fly) or whatever makes geckos good at climbing.

objects.title: Balance/Coordination
objects.text: This is where you can get yourself a tail. Tails can situationally also function as an extra limb.

objects.title: Natural Weapon
objects.text: This is where you can get an in-built natural weapon, like fangs or retractable claws.

objects.title: Durability
objects.text: This is where you can become more durable, such as by having scales in select areas.

objects.title: Environmental Adaptation
objects.text: This is where you get your situationally incredible boosts, like gills for underwater breathing.

objects.title: Exotic
objects.text: This is the miscellaneous category for venom, color changing skin, or some other thing I'm struggling to think of.

objects.title: Other
objects.text: If I missed a category of trait that you want, such as replacing limbs to have digitigrade legs or a mermaid tail, then use this.

rows.title: Faunus Trait
rows.titleText: This lets you choose a Faunus trait you want, though note that this is a genetic trait, so you'll be stuck with what you get. The Faunus trait is normally only an addition to pre-existing biology, with the trait being up to you. Simply choose what you want the trait to primarily boost and then determine what specific animal you have a trait of. Every Faunus trait is visible. For some reason, night vision is a common freebie, so you're guaranteed to have it.

objects.title: Partner
objects.text: This is for a build made using the Partner perk. Give yourself the ammount of points you're meant to have based on the main build.

rows.title: Difficulty
rows.titleText: The difficulties here come with pros and cons. On Hard Mode, you are guaranteed to instantly start with your semblance, whatever it ends up being. On Normal Mode, you're guaranteed to unlock it within a year of your insertion. On Easy Mode, your aura is already unlocked at insertion, your semblance is available immediately, and you've got a small head-start on training to boot — basically, the world is your oyster. Partner Mode follows the same rule as Normal Mode. Legend Mode isn't so much a difficulty as it is a request for more: each purchase adds bonus points on top of your chosen difficulty, no questions asked and no upper limit.

objects.title: Atlas
objects.text: The home to the world's greatest military and the Schnee Dust Company, Remnant's most powerful corporation, Atlas is based loosely on Germany, Russia, and Scandinavia.

objects.title: Mistral
objects.text: Vaguely based on East Asia and the Mediterranean, Mistral has diverse ecosystems, a focus on trade/the arts, and is the most racist place in Remnant.

objects.title: Vacuo
objects.text: A desert nation based on the Middle East, Vacuo's people are hardy, valuing survival and freedom above most else.

objects.title: Vale
objects.text: This is the stable (boring) Kingdom. Being based on the USA and seemingly also Western Europe, Vale's culture is that of a melting pot.

objects.title: Menagerie
objects.text: As one might infer from the racially motivated name, this is the home to a large number of the world's Faunus population. It is also two thirds inhospitable desert.

objects.title: Other
objects.text: Maybe you want to be in the wilderness somewhere, an unnamed village, or Salem's lair. Either way, you're not going to a standard location.

rows.title: Starting Area
rows.titleText: Where will your journey begin? Each of these kingdoms (none of them have a monarchy anymore) is unique in their culture, environment, and problems. Below, you can choose between one of the four kingdoms, or somewhere else entirely, with the expectation being that you start in the capital of wherever you'll be.

objects.title: BlazBlue
objects.text: A chaotic, post-apocalyptic world stuck in a timeloop where magic, technology, and otherworldly powers interact. You can choose to start in the final loop.

objects.title: Persona
objects.text: A world similar to modern Earth, but where the human unconscious shapes hidden worlds that create monsters and offer power to those who accept themselves fully.

objects.title: Under Night In-Birth
objects.text: An urban fantasy setting centered around the Hollow Night, wherein people can control a force created by human perception.

objects.title: Other
objects.text: This is your option for if you want to go to another world entirely.

rows.title: Other Worlds
rows.titleText: Maybe you don't want to be on Remnant and are looking for something a bit more interesting? Here's where you can do exactly that. You can choose to have your powers adapt to that setting or simply remain as they are now, making you a being nobody can truly account for. Yes, I chose these worlds because they're in BBTAG. Feel free to start whenever you want.

objects.title: Canon
objects.text: This is the time period that the story takes place in, where the stalemate between Ozpin and Salem is at risk of being broken.

objects.title: STRQ Era
objects.text: Roughly 20 years precanon, this is when Team STRQ (read as Stark) were students at Beacon academy, before 3 of them became parents to both Ruby Rose and Yang Xiao Long a few years later.

objects.title: Faunus Revolution
objects.text: Roughly 30 years precanon, this is the war fought by the Faunus to resist Humans trying to force them all to relocate to the island of Menagerie. The Faunus won, but the war's impact lingers.

objects.title: Great War
objects.text: Roughly 80-90 years precanon, this is the 10-year conflict between the four kingdoms that lead to the current political landscape and the foundation of the huntsman academies.

objects.title: Other
objects.text: This could be any other era, such as the time when Salem was still wandering the world alone, before the gods brought Ozma into their nonsense, or when magic still existed.

rows.title: Time Period
rows.titleText: You can start up to a year before or after any of the events in your chosen time period.

objects.title: Retcon
objects.text: With each purchase, you can make one change that would have no meaningful effect on the plot or world. Such changes would include making a character less cringe, the main cast older by a few years, or the later season outfits being on par with the early ones.

objects.title: Genderbend
objects.text: You can swap one named character of your choice from male or female to the other, as long as you make an opposite swap to another named character. The swapped characters must be of roughly equal plot relevance. You can make one swap per purchase.

objects.title: Strings of Fate
objects.text: Fate itself will go the same was as canon, barring world mods and missions that might directly impact it. Unless you or another person brought into this world by this cyoa, including by drawbacks, intentionally interfere with canon events, they will go the exact same way.

objects.title: Pyrrha Lives
objects.text: Isn't it a tragedy that the Invincible Girl died so young? Yes, it certainly helped Jaune Arc grow as a person and huntsman, but what if he died instead of her? In this world, Jaune will die a hero, saving Pyrrha Nikos from her fate in Volume 3.

objects.title: Flexible Aura
objects.text:
The capabilities of Aura are now even broader, not that they needed to be. Now, explosive bursts of aura for propulsion, creating after images, and even injecting it into Grimm as an attack are all possible, but only at a high level of skill.

This makes new options available in the Aura section.

objects.title: Jaune Fic
objects.text: No matter what you do, the world will guarantee that Jaune Arc is born and that the narrative of the world warps to heavily involve him. If he goes to Atlas because Beacon rejects him, then his team will do covert operations to stop Salem's operatives. If he makes a coffeeshop, he'll talk no jutsu Cinder. The world will be better off, but do you really want Jaune to be the reason why?

objects.title: Fanfic Tropes
objects.text: With each selection, you can add one fanfic trope to the world, such as Jaune Arc having a harem (please don't do this to Pyrrha), Ironwood treating Penny like a daughter, or having one person suddenly gain intimate knowledge of the future.

addons.title: No Restrictions
addons.text: Fine, you can now make any ship canon, insofar as it doesn't require bringing anyone back from the dead.

objects.title: Shipping
objects.text: There's a whole ship chart, so I assume some of you want this. This just lets you make whatever ships you like canon, insofar as they don't require bringing someone back from the dead or making anyone swap sides. You can roughly determine how the relationship starts, such as who initiates and why.

objects.title: Eternal Queen
objects.text: Instead of gaining the power of controlling Grimm, Salem instead founded a kingdom of her own in the dragon-shaped continent of Evernight, uncreatively called Evernight. This kingdom is ruled by their Goddess, Salem, whose magic is believed to be a gift from The Brothers. Her people consider "Ozma" to be an enemy of humanity to scheme against and are known for their expertise in the manipulation of Aura.

objects.title: Betrayed King
objects.text: Ozma, more often known as Ozpin, his current incarnation, instead of being the kind, shady man he once was, has been driven past the point of trusting others. He's been betrayed far too many times to expect people to stay on his side when they learn their enemy is unkillable, so he manipulates people into working for him instead. This means that Raven is still working for him, though she still abandoned her family.

objects.title: Born From Negativity
objects.text: How troubling. Grimm can form from large amounts of localized negative emotions, meaning that wars, hate mobs, and other such gatherings of negativity can result in Grimm manifesting in nearby shadows. This has resulted in a world that is kinder by necessity, with governments doing everything in their power to prevent negativity. Also, large tragedies and disasters snowball into even larger ones, so civilization is in an even less stable position.

objects.title: Weirder Grimm
objects.text: Grimm normally just directly try to kill people, but now there's several somewhat uncommon kinds that have various mental effects, such as causing feelings of love to significantly intensify, intensifying tendency towards negativity, outright nullifying someone's capacity to feel joy for some amount of time, or even stranger effects.

addons.title: Extra Trait
addons.text: By selecting this, you can get up to 2 extra traits from your chosen animal.

objects.title: Multiple Traits
objects.text: Faunus can actually have multiple animal traits, with some having upwards of 3-5 traits.

objects.title: Hereditary
objects.text: People who are given magic can pass down weaker versions to their children, resulting in Yang having the eyesight of a raven and a small handful of Maidens in history having children with notably powerful semblances, some of which are hereditary. These abilities can be passed down further generations.

objects.title: No Longer Immortal
objects.text: In this world, Salem's curse of immortality has actually faded with time, reducing her to mere agelessness. While she is no longer unkillable, she is not aware of this and will still try to collect the relics and make The Brothers to end her curse by destroying the world.

rows.title: World Modifiers
rows.titleText: This world could use some changes, no? Here's where you get to make them. Changes will cost or give points depending on how they affect the world.

objects.title: Beauty
objects.text: You're no model, but you're a solid 8/10 and your ideal physique will be pretty easy to get into and maintain. This probably won't help in fights, but it'll help plenty outside of them.

objects.title: Perfect
objects.text: Most would see you as a perfect 10/10. You will age gracefully and your injuries will heal such that you're still attractive. You'll likely end up with a fair few fans from your looks alone.

objects.title: Self Sufficiency
objects.text: Life can be a slog, and a painful one if you don't know what you're doing. Now, you can handle stuff like taxes, cooking for yourself, etc. with ease. This also lets you mentally handle the boredom that comes with a mundane life.

objects.title: Instinct
objects.text: You were born for battle. Unlike most, you are fairly capable in a fight without any training, though your lack of experience will mean skilled fighters can beat you fairly easily.

objects.title: Golden Rule
objects.text: You will have no difficulties finding money, as though the world itself wants you to be wealthy. Whether it be gambling luck, investments being likely to pay off, or just finding money on the ground. Most people will envy this.

objects.title: High Willpower
objects.text: Most people would eventually be broken down by the cruelty heroes are often exposed to, but you? You are one of the few who will never be broken down. As you see your loved ones hurt, as the innocent suffer while you are too weak to help, and as your own life crumbles, you will always have the resolve to move forward.

objects.title: Fated Encounter
objects.text: As long as it's possible, you are guaranteed to meet one individual of your choosing from canon or the mission section and have some degree of control over the interaction. This does not guarantee how they'll see you.

objects.title: Charisma
objects.text: You'll have an innate knack for making friends and swaying people to your side. Even your enemies are likely to gain some respect towards you. In fact, you'd be able to make a friend out of nearly anyone not meaningfully opposed to you.

objects.title: True Friends
objects.text: The people you keep close won't just be friends, but people with whom you can build deep enough bonds that you might as well have a second family. Your capacity for fighting as a team with your chosen Teammates will be considerably increased.

objects.title: Item Insurance
objects.text: Fate itself has been adjusted so if your items purchased in this cyoa are lost, stolen, or damaged, events will play out so that they will be eventually back in your hands. They will never be left so damaged that they can't be repaired. This applies to also applies to the weapon from the Weapon section

objects.title: Signature Look
objects.text: This is a world where people tend to be pretty stylish (and easy to cosplay), so you'd better look like you fit in, right? This will make you instinctively know how to style yourself and have luck finding good clothes.

objects.title: Fast Learner
objects.text: Your rate of growth as a fighter is truly impressive. In mere days, you'll improve to a degree that'd take most upwards of a month.

objects.title: Beginner's Luck
objects.text: No matter what it is you do, when attempting it for the first time, you'll do decently at it. This would mean something like giving a vague, but not exactly incorrect answer when being asked a question, or being able to fight at the level of a huntsman in training when using a weapon for the first time.

objects.title: Schnee Semblance
objects.text: You're a member of the Schnee family by blood, letting you inherit their semblance. This allows you to create glyphs which can serve as solid platforms and have repelling/attracting forces, summon icy avatars of previously killed enemies, and combine the glyphs with dust for elemental effects.

objects.title: Soundtrack
objects.text: RWBY has very few virtues as a show and the music is one of them. You now have a mental playlist of all music in RWBY that you can hear on command.

objects.title: Conversion
objects.text: Each purchase turns 1 point into 2 lien, in case you wanted more items and weapon upgrades.

objects.title: Partner
objects.text: Having a partner would be a useful thing for any huntsman, right? By selecting this, you can make another build starting with twice the number of points that you put into this perk. You are guaranteed to work well with your partner. Your partner cannot make a partner of their own. You can choose if your partner will be a character of your creation or someone random.

objects.title: Meta
objects.text: If you choose this then you can use a meta cyoa as a starting point for your build.

You can eat just about anything without consequence. Spoiled food, suspicious street meat, Mystery Meat Friday at the academy cafeteria — none of it will so much as give you gas. You're also highly resistant to most ingested poisons, which is more useful than you'd think in a world where some people still fight with poisoned daggers.
objects.title: Iron Stomach

objects.text: You remember everything you've ever seen, heard, or read with perfect clarity. Skim a textbook once and you'll recall any page on demand. Spot a White Fang member's face in a crowd and you'll never forget it. The downside is that you also remember every embarrassing thing you've ever done — but that's a small price to pay.
objects.title: Photographic Memory

objects.text: Pain that would buckle most people barely registers to you. A broken arm is annoying, not debilitating. A serious wound is something you can fight through for at least a few more minutes. This doesn't make you any harder to actually injure, just much better at staying functional when you are.
objects.title: Pain Tolerance

objects.text: You can get a read on people with a glance. Their general emotional state, whether they're lying, what they actually want versus what they're saying — none of it is hidden from you. This isn't mind reading; you're just very good at reading micro-expressions, body language, and the thousand little tells people don't know they have. Liars hate you.
objects.title: Soul Read

objects.text: Once per week, you can call on a stroke of narrative luck. The Beowolf trips over a root at the right moment. The guard happens to be looking the other way. Your shot ricochets off a rock and into the switch. It won't save you from a genuinely bad plan, but it can salvage a borderline one. Don't push your luck — it knows when you're being greedy.
objects.title: Lucky Star

objects.text: Nameless mooks, common Grimm, and random bandits cannot kill you. They can wound you, inconvenience you, and ruin your day — but a stray shot from a White Fang mook will not, under any circumstances, end your story. Named characters, on the other hand, can absolutely still end you. So maybe don't pick a fight with Tyrian.
objects.title: Plot Armor

objects.text: When multiple enemies engage you at once, they'll politely attack one at a time, waiting their turn like it's a boss fight. This doesn't apply to intelligent foes working as a coordinated team — White Fang elites, huntsmen, etc. — but your average bandit camp will suddenly discover a deep cultural tradition of single combat.
objects.title: Mook Chivalry

objects.text: Your senses have decided to share notes. You can faintly see aura even with your eyes closed, hear movement as a faint shimmer of color, and smell hostile intent. It's disorienting at first, but once you adjust, you'll have an almost 360-degree awareness of anything trying to kill you. Loud noises are now a personal offense.
objects.title: Combat Synesthesia

objects.text: One canon character of your choosing will take you under their wing, training you in their particular style and sharing their hard-won wisdom. This is a genuine mentorship, not a one-off — they'll stick around, give advice, and occasionally drag you out of trouble. Choose wisely; you don't get to swap later.
objects.title: Mentor

objects.text: You arrive in Remnant with a reputation of your choosing, good or bad. Maybe you're known as a tournament prodigy, the survivor of a famous Grimm attack, or the illegitimate child of a councilor. People will have heard of you and have opinions before you've even met. Choose something that opens doors you want open.
objects.title: Reputation

objects.text:
Your mind operates on a level few can hope to match.

Your memory is exceptional, your reasoning razor-sharp, and your ability to recognize patterns, learn new disciplines, and solve complex problems borders on the unbelievable. Ideas come naturally, connections others would miss become obvious, and subjects that take years to master can often be understood in a fraction of the time.
objects.title: +50 To Your IQ

objects.text: A wealthy individual — a councilor, an SDC executive, an old-money heir, your pick — has decided you're worth investing in. They'll cover your expenses, pull strings when needed, and ask for favors in return roughly twice a year. The favors will be legal, if occasionally morally flexible.
objects.title: Patron

objects.text: You've lived before. Faint memories of past lives surface in dreams and déjà vu — a swordsman's instinct here, a scholar's trivia there. You won't get full flashbacks, but you'll occasionally just know things you have no business knowing. Whether this is reincarnation, genetic memory, or something weirder is up to you.
objects.title: Old Soul

objects.text: You have contacts in the criminal underworld of every kingdom. Fences, smugglers, informants, the occasional corrupt official — they'll do business with you, give you a heads-up on heat coming your way, and never, ever snitch. They're not your friends. They're professionals. Don't insult them by treating them otherwise.
objects.title: Friends in Low Places

objects.text: You have connections in the upper crust of at least one kingdom's government. You can get meetings with councilors, pull strings for permits and licenses, and call in the occasional favor from people whose names appear in the news. These favors are not unlimited and they will be remembered.
objects.title: Friends in High Places

objects.text: You survived something you shouldn't have. A Grimm attack, a bandit raid, a Fall of Beacon-tier catastrophe — pick your tragedy. The experience left its marks, but it also left you with the kind of calm-under-pressure that only comes from having already seen the worst. New threats will have a hard time impressing you.
objects.title: Survivor

objects.text: You've won at least one major tournament in your past, and the footage is still floating around. Other huntsmen will recognize your name, sponsors will send you free gear, and your opponents will take you seriously from the opening bell. Just hope nobody's been studying your tape.
objects.title: Tournament Champion

objects.text: Animals just like you. Wild creatures won't flee from you, domestic ones will flock to you, and trained attack dogs will hesitate before following a 'sic him' command. This doesn't work on Grimm — they're not really animals — but it's still a nice perk for anyone who spends time in the wilderness.
objects.title: Animal Affinity
Unlucky
objects.text: You'll simply have bad luck. The first purchase will make you unlucky in minor ways outside of combat, such as your train being late. The second purchase will also make you unlucky in combat, such as tripping or having the sun in your eyes. This will not be debilitating, but it is guaranteed to inconvenience you in your day to day life.

objects.title: What's This?
objects.text: You will not remember your build, or any of the decisions you've made, meaning you'll know little more than your own past and the bare minimum information to use your powers.

objects.title: Unskilled
objects.text: You will not start out with the capabilities outlined in your chosen skills, instead reaching that level slowly over the course of six months.

objects.title: Hero's Journey
objects.text: The world will ensure that your journey is an interesting one. As you grow, you will encounter bigger and harder to solve problems, until eventually you reach full mastery of your abilities, after which things will settle down and return to normalcy. This will guarantee that you are dragged into the main plot.

objects.title: Player 2
objects.text: There is now someone entering the world at the same time as you who also has a build from this cyoa that includes this drawback. They will be using the same difficulty as you. By default, you have no clue who they are or what their goals are.

objects.title: There's More?
objects.text: There's now at least 5-10 other people entering this world with you. They'll be using the same difficulty as you along with this drawback, if they even have a build from this cyoa at all. Have fun planning around these unknown elements.

objects.title: Cross of Fire
objects.text: You now have an enemy truly difficult to prepare for. The other person entering this world is someone who is morally opposed to you in such a way that will result in becoming enemies once you meet. No matter how many people come with you to this world, there will only be one such enemy like this. If there are multiple others entering, this will by default only apply to one, though you can have this apply to however many more you wish.

objects.title: Disability
objects.text: You lose either an eye or a limb for each time you select this.

objects.title: Weakened
objects.text: You will be starting at half of your full power, with your purchased power being restored slowly over the next 24 months.

objects.title: Starting From Nothing
objects.text: You will be starting with none of your purchased power, needing to unlock it yourself over time. Thankfully, you'll grow to unlock it twice as fast as you otherwise would.

objects.title: Random Semblance
objects.text: You won't be able to build a semblance for yourself in the Semblance section. This does not stop you from unlocking a random semblance later, which will be based on your personality in some way.

objects.title: No Semblance
objects.text: Unfortunately, you simply will not be able to unlock your semblance at all. For what it's worth, many combatants, such as Mercury Black, Roman Torchwick, and James Ironwood, don't use or need a semblance to fight, so you won't either.

objects.title: Semblance Delay
objects.text: You're actually guaranteed to not unlock your semblance for a full year, after which you'll unlock it based on your difficulty. After that year, you'll either instantly unlock it if you're on Hard Mode, or unlock it sometime within the next year if you're on Normal.

objects.title: Blue Chip
objects.text: Both Ozpin and Salem are guaranteed to find out about your abilities within a year of your insertion, so expect them to try to recruit you, or kill you if you choose the other side. They won't know for a fact whether your abilities include magic, but they might suspect it.

objects.title: No Aura
objects.text: You start without your aura unlocked. This isn't a very big deal for most, but for a huntsman, this is a comical disadvantage because you have no experience with any aura technique or your semblance. If on Hard mode, you'll get your semblance when you unlock your aura.

objects.title: Bad Design
objects.text: Unlike the stylish, memorable, easy to cosplay designs from Volumes one through three, your sense of fashion is questionable, to say the least. That is to say, you struggle to dress yourself in a way that's particularly stylish.

objects.title: Source?
objects.text: Your metaknowledge won't be taken seriously unless you can prove the things you know. Make sure not to go blab about the lady controlling the Grimm because people won't believe you.

objects.title: What Show?
objects.text: You have lost all knowledge of RWBY and the World of Remnant beyond what certain reincarnation options may let you know, except for the most basic information required to understand your build choices.

objects.title: Can't Hide
objects.text: Unlike some Faunus, you either physically cannot or simply refuse to hide your animal trait. You are almost guaranteed to be a victim of regular racism because of this.

objects.title: Racist
objects.text: Weiss, is that you? Regardless of your race, you'll believe that the other is inferior. If you're a human, you'll believe that faunus are vaguely inferior in some way, but if you're a faunus, then you'll believe the opposite. You can learn to stop being racist, but it'll take time. Until then, this will mostly manifest in subtle ways.

Someone wants you dead. Not a vague enemy, not a faceless organization — a specific person with the resources to make a serious attempt at least once a month. Could be a crime boss, a corrupt official, a vengeful huntsman. You won't know who they are until they make their first move.
objects.title: Wanted

objects.text: You owe a substantial amount of money to people who don't take 'I'll pay you next week' for an answer. The exact amount and creditor are up to you, but expect monthly visits from large men with poorly-concealed weapons. Working as a huntsman pays well, so this is more annoyance than ruin — but skipping payments is not an option.
objects.title: Debt

objects.text: You have a paralyzing fear of something common in a huntsman's life. Could be heights, fire, enclosed spaces, blood, the ocean — pick your poison. You'll need to make a serious effort to push through it, and you won't be at your best when you do. Most huntsmen have one of these. Yours is just worse.
objects.title: Phobia

objects.text: A past injury never fully healed. Maybe it's a knee that locks up in cold weather, a shoulder that dislocates at the worst times, or a back that gives out after long fights. Aura keeps it from getting worse, but it's a permanent weak point and a smart enemy will notice.
objects.title: Old Injury

objects.text: One of your senses is significantly impaired. You're partially deaf, colorblind, have a weak sense of smell, or something similar. Aura won't fix it. Pick which sense and how bad — but it has to be bad enough to actually affect your life, not just be a minor inconvenience.
objects.title: Dulled Senses

objects.text: You can't remember the last time you slept more than four hours. Your aura recovers slower as a result, your temper is shorter, and you occasionally zone out at the worst possible moment. Stimulants help, but only so much. The bags under your eyes are now a permanent accessory.
objects.title: Insomniac

objects.text: You can't bring yourself to fully trust anyone. You'll second-guess your teammates' decisions, hoard information 'just in case,' and assume the worst when someone does something nice for you. Forming the deep bonds that power team coordination is going to be a slow, painful process — if it happens at all.
objects.title: Trust Issues

objects.text: Someone in your inner circle — a teammate, a mentor, a romantic interest — will eventually betray you. You don't know who, and neither do they (yet). The betrayal will be serious enough to derail whatever you were working toward at the time. The only thing you can do is try to be the kind of person nobody wants to betray.
objects.title: Traitor

objects.text: Salem has personally noticed you and decided you'd make a fine addition to her collection. She won't send her whole army after you — that would be wasteful — but expect her agents to make regular recruitment pitches, with the implicit 'or else' hanging over each one. Refusing has consequences. Accepting is worse.
objects.title: Salem's Mark

objects.text: You're a celebrity. Your face is on the news, your love life is in the tabloids, and your every huntsman mission gets live-commentated by some scroll personality. Going incognito is basically impossible, and your mistakes will be very public. The upside is that your successes are too — but they're downplayed and your failures get amplified.
objects.title: Public Figure

objects.text: There's a kingdom-wide warrant out for your arrest for a crime you didn't commit. The real culprit is either dead, missing, or genuinely believes you did it. You can clear your name eventually, but until then, every interaction with law enforcement is a coin flip and your face is on wanted posters across Remnant.
objects.title: Wanted Criminal

objects.text: You've sworn a solemn oath that constrains your behavior — never kill, never lie, never abandon a teammate, protect a specific person, etc. Pick something that will actually come up. Breaking the vow won't instantly kill you, but you'll suffer appropriate supernatural backlash: bad luck, weakened aura, nightmares, etc., until you atone.
objects.title: Vow

objects.text: You're severely allergic to something common in a huntsman's life. Could be a type of dust, a common medication, a food that shows up at every academy cafeteria, or something else. Exposure triggers a reaction that aura can mitigate but not prevent. Carry an epinephrine scroll at all times.
objects.title: Allergy

objects.text: Your rate of growth as a fighter is roughly half what it should be. Things that take others a week to learn will take you two, and your ceiling is lower to boot. This is a serious problem in a world where power scaling matters. On the plus side, you'll never be accused of being a Mary Sue.
objects.title: Slow Learner

objects.text: Your aura protects you from injury as well as anyone's, but it does a poor job of protecting you from being knocked out. Solid hits to the head will drop you faster than they should, and you're particularly vulnerable to flashbangs, sonic weapons, and anything else that rattles the brain. Wear a helmet.
objects.title: Glass Jaw

objects.text: Grimm are unusually drawn to you. Not just because of negative emotions — though you have plenty of those — but something about you smells like a beacon. Expect roughly twice as many Grimm encounters as a typical huntsman, including the occasional one that shows up in places it really shouldn't.
objects.title: Grimm Magnet

objects.text: A specific organization — the White Fang, the SDC, a kingdom's military, a huntsman academy — has it out for you. Members will go out of their way to make your life difficult, from paperwork games to outright sabotage. They won't openly attack you (usually), but every interaction with them will be hostile.
objects.title: Hated Faction

objects.text: A magical taboo has been placed on you. Pick something specific — never break bread with a faunus, never draw your weapon on a Sunday, never refuse a request from a child, etc. The taboo is enforced supernaturally; breaking it triggers immediate, severe bad luck that lasts until you complete a suitable quest to lift the curse.
objects.title: Geas

objects.text: You're known for something shameful. Maybe you ran from a fight, maybe you got caught cheating at the Vytal Festival, maybe you're the huntsman who 'accidentally' leveled a village. Whatever it is, the story is more dramatic than the truth, and people will recognize you for it. Earning respect will require proving yourself over and over.
objects.title: Infamy

objects.text: Your powers — semblance, magic, anything purchased in this cyoa — weaken the further you get from a chosen location. Within a city block of your anchor, you're at full strength. Cross a kingdom border, and you're at maybe 60%. Leave the kingdom entirely, and you're a regular (if well-trained) human. Pick your anchor carefully.
objects.title: Anchored

rows.title: Drawbacks
rows.titleText: Unless otherwise specified, drawbacks will override other options where applicable.

objects.title: Shallow Pool
objects.text: Your reserves are noticeably lower than most, but to make up for it, your aura is easy to control and fully regenerates much more quickly.

objects.title: Standard Pool
objects.text: This marks your reserves as average, which means you'll be able to take as many hits as most people.

objects.title: Large Pool
objects.text: Most Huntsmen would be impressed by your sheer reserves. You're not a walking tank by any means, but you're more durable than most.

objects.title: Deep Reservoir
objects.text: Just like Jaune Arc, your reserves are deeper than would make sense. Weaker opponents might struggle to deal any damage at all.

objects.title: Inexperienced
objects.text: When it comes to using aura, you know so little that it's a miracle you know what it is in the first place. Without concentrating, you won't consistently be able to protect yourself with aura.

objects.title: Standard Efficiency
objects.text: Your proficiency with aura is roughly comparable to a huntsman in training. Advanced techniques are likely still difficult for you. When you see an attack coming, you can reflexively activate your aura to stop it.

objects.title: Refined Efficiency
objects.text: Your efficiency is on the level that a normal huntsman is expected to reach. It's reasonable to expect knowing an advanced technique or two. Your aura is activated subconsciously, protecting you from attacks too fast for you to consciously respond to.

objects.title: Perfect Efficiency
objects.text: You waste almost zero aura. Techniques are less costly, and you can take more hits than your capacity might indicate. Your aura is always active, protecting you from sneak attacks.

Your aura regenerates noticeably slower than most. A fight that ends with you on empty will leave you sitting on the sidelines for twice as long as your teammates. On the upside, you've gotten very good at conserving what you have.
objects.title: Sluggish Recovery

objects.text: Your aura recovers at a typical rate — fast enough to be back in fighting shape after a short rest, slow enough that you can't just outlast a prolonged engagement. Nothing to write home about, but no real weakness either.
objects.title: Standard Recovery

objects.text: Your aura snaps back fast. A five-minute breather restores what would take most huntsmen an hour, and you can fight through mild attrition that would wear others down. Just don't get cocky — fast recovery isn't infinite recovery.
objects.title: Fast Recovery

objects.text: Your aura bounces back almost as fast as you spend it. In a fight, this means you can trade blows in ways that would bankrupt most huntsmen. Out of one, it means you're always the one volunteering for the night watch. The downside: this much regeneration makes you very hungry.
objects.title: Nigh-Instant Recovery

objects.text: Your aura extends no further than your skin. You can reinforce your body, but you can't project aura outward at all — no aura pulses, no reinforcement of held objects beyond what touching them naturally provides. In exchange, your internal control is exceptional.
objects.title: Touch Reach

objects.text: Your aura extends a short distance from your body — enough to reinforce a held weapon and soften blows that would otherwise connect. This is what most huntsmen work with.
objects.title: Standard Reach

objects.text: Your aura extends a meter or so from your body, enough to reinforce a polearm's full length or push back a charging Ursa without bracing. You can project small aura pulses for close-range defense.
objects.title: Extended Reach

objects.text: You can project your aura outward as visible constructs — shields, blades, simple shapes — within roughly five meters. These aren't semblance-tier effects, just raw aura shaped by will, but a projected aura shield can stop a Beowolf's lunge cold.
objects.title: Aura Projection

rows.title: Aura
rows.titleText: Aura is the main magic system in Remnant, with its primary purposes being the way you power your semblance and a forcefield of sorts that protects you from harm, though not from things like knockback or the pain from an attack. This is where you'll determine the amount of it you have, as well as exactly how well you can use it. In addition to this, it will power your semblance and provide a few other benefits. Using aura efficiently is a skill you'll need to learn, so don't bank on just having enough aura to tank attacks. While increasing one's aura capacity via training is possible, it's broadly a better use of your time to increase your proficiency with it and/or become a better fighter.

objects.title: Enhanced Healing
objects.text: Seemingly just be base a part of aura, you can channel it into wounds to make them heal more quickly, though you can't heal from wounds that'd normally be permanent.

objects.title: Enhanced Strength
objects.text: One of the most common skills with Aura, you can use aura to enhance your body for striking, lifting, and other feats of strength. This is fully conscious, so you won't accidentally break things.

objects.title: Sensing Aura
objects.text: You can feel the Auras of yourself and others, such as when Lie Ren sensed a powerful, hostile warrior approaching him. Without this, you can't tell whether you're running low on aura or not.

objects.title: Survey
objects.text: In addition to sensing the auras of others, you can send out pulses of aura to clearly "see" things within 10m, with anything past becoming increasingly blurry.

objects.title: Aura Unlock
objects.text: Much like Pyrrha Nikos, you can unlock someone else's Aura, letting them gain access to both it and a chance to unlock a random semblance. This seems to require a personalized chant to do.

objects.title: Item Channeling
objects.text: You can channel aura through things, enhancing their durability, though it's less effective than enhancing your own body.

objects.title: Projectile Slashes
objects.text: You can condense aura channeled through a blade letting it become a projectile slash or thrust when you attack, letting you turn melee attacks into ranged ones.

objects.title: Mastered Slashes
objects.text: At your level of mastery, cutting down steel beams from over 10 meters away is entirely reasonable. Some will assume this is your semblance.

objects.title: Run and Jump
objects.text: By channeling Aura through your legs and into the ground, reinforcing both, you can run and jump both considerably higher and faster than is normally possible without damaging the ground.

objects.title: Skate
objects.text: By precisely applying aura to a surface or the part of your body making contact to it, you can temporarily nullify friction for the affected area, letting you achieve controlled sliding/skating. It is relatively difficult to use this on uneven surfaces.

objects.title: Crude Constructs
objects.text: Much like Lie Ren in exactly one scene in Volume 1, you can made crude constructs out of aura, such as a circular disk, or other simple shapes. While not durable, these do have some use.

objects.title: Refined Constructs
objects.text: Your aura constructs are now durable enough to be used as weapons. Though they can only be one continuous shape, this lets you make things like weapons and shields, so you'll never be truly unarmed as long as you have aura left. This is fairly aura intensive, so it's best used in case of being disarmed rather than in place of a weapon.

objects.title: Energy Blasts
objects.text: You can concentrate balls of aura in your hand to fire at others with enough force to dent a steel door. This isn't exactly efficient, but it serves as a ranged option when you otherwise don't have any.

objects.title: Spiral
objects.text: Now you can apply a fast rotational force to a ball of aura, letting it serve as a devastating, if notably inefficient melee attack.

objects.title: Scattershot
objects.text: Instead of one large ball of aura, you can make them smaller and fire up to 10 at once, letting it function like a shotgun blast.

objects.title: Dust Infusion
objects.text: You can combine your external uses of aura with dust, resulting in elemental effects. This can include dust weaved into your clothing.

rows.title: Techniques
rows.titleText: Aura's uses aren't quite as straight forward as you might think. Skilled practitioners are able to do a lot more than most would expect, with this section letting you choose what techniques you'll be starting with the knowledge of and the capacity to use. Thankfully, some things are so simple that they're shown as even being possible for civilians.

objects.title: Injection
objects.text: You can push your Aura into someone else offensively, disrupting the flow of their own Aura, making it harder to use. This deals significant internal damage to Grimm.

objects.title: Burst
objects.text: Much like Yang's semblance, you can channel your Aura to, through your attacks, produce bursts of concussive force, even without dust.

objects.title: Directional Blast
objects.text: Those same bursts of force can be used with much greater ease, letting you use them to propel yourself through and change your direction in the air.

objects.title: Echo
objects.text: You can leave a brief, stationary imprint of your Aura where you currently are. This is only useful for fighting those who can sense your Aura.

objects.title: Afterimage
objects.text: The brief imprint of your aura can also mimic your actual appearance, being so close that most won't immediately realize that it's an afterimage. With training, you can learn to make it move after its creation, doing things like following through with an attack while you disengage.

objects.title: Rough
objects.text: You can project your aura outward with a rougher texture, similar to sandpaper. This can be used to enhance your strikes and even let an aura infused blade function similarly to a saw.

rows.title: Expanded Techniques
rows.titleText: By choosing Flexible Aura, you've unlocked some extra Aura techniques. These can be expanded on by a skilled user, just like the ones above. This section is mostly just for techniques I wanted to include in the first section but felt were too far removed from how Aura seems to work in canon.

objects.title: Range - Touch
objects.text: Your semblance is only effective on your own body and at touch range.

objects.title: Range - Melee
objects.text: Your semblance is effective on anything within 2 meters of your body, which is roughly 6.5 feet.

objects.title: Range - Medium
objects.text: Your semblance is effective on anything within 25 meters of your body, which is roughly 82 feet.

objects.title: Range - Long
objects.text: Your semblance's range limit is an entire 100 meters, which is 328 feet.

objects.title: Usability - Subconscious
objects.text: Your semblance is activated and used without any conscious input. When a certain condition is met, the semblance activates on its own. For the sake of the cyoa, you can make a onetime choice for what the condition is.

objects.title: Usability - Concentration
objects.text: Your semblance can be used consciously, though it requires serious, full concentration, making it harder to use during combat.

objects.title: Usability - Trigger
objects.text: Your semblance is activated by a mental trigger, making it as easy to activate your semblance as it is to snap your fingers.

objects.title: Usability - Instinctual
objects.text: Your semblance is either always active or requires no effort to use. Using your semblance is as easy as breathing.

objects.title: Cost - Extreme
objects.text: Your semblance drains aura very quickly and is best left as a last resort or finisher.

objects.title: Cost - High
objects.text: Your semblance needs to be used sparingly, only seeing use a few times in any given fight.

objects.title: Cost - Moderate
objects.text: Your semblance is draining enough to still require pacing, but you could use it in a fight without much issue.

objects.title: Cost - Low
objects.text: Your semblance can be used fairly freely without much concern, both in and out of combat.

objects.title: Dust Compatible
objects.text: Your semblance, much like the Schnee's glyph semblance, can be integrated with dust to create a variety of additional elemental effects.

objects.title: Hereditary
objects.text: Just like the Schnee family semblance, yours is passed down from generation to generation. If you're an insert, this means it's possible for direct relatives to have the semblance and knowledge on how to use it better.

objects.title: Evolution
objects.text: Sometime within a year of unlocking it, your semblance is guaranteed to evolve, unlocking new abilities or directly becoming stronger.

rows.title: Semblance
rows.titleText:
This is your special power that's unique (unless otherwise specified), (probably) flashy, and tied to your soul as well as personality. These are powered by aura, though passive effects and effects that exclusively affect peoples' minds are normally less aura intensive, with people like Qrow Branwen and Emeral Sustrai being able to maintain their semblances indefinitely. This is where you choose the foundation of your semblance. Effects are in a secondary section below.

If it warrants mentioning, you cannot receive the semblance you build here if you choose a perk or drawback that gives you a different semblance or stops you from getting one.

objects.title: Elemental
objects.text: With each selection of this, you can choose one of the following elements for your semblance to manipulate: air, earth, fire, and water. Fire can be created for the purpose of the semblance, unlike the other elements.

objects.title: Telekinetic
objects.text: You can move objects within range as well as you could move them with your body, both by strength and by dexterity. This is capable of controlling finer mechanisms of objects, as long as you know of and can see/sense them.

objects.title: Pocket
objects.text: You can open up a portal in the air into a pocket dimension with roughly 10 cubic meters of space inside, where time flows as it normally would. You can control the size of the portal. Objects partially inside the dimension will be ejected if the portal closes.

objects.title: Resonance
objects.text: When focusing, you can control sounds within range, whether by dampening or amplifying them, which can be easily used for stealth, but also attacks.

objects.title: Conduit
objects.text: You can control electricity within range, such as stimulating your body, or discharging it for attacks. This control is boosted while moving. In addition, you can absorb electricity harmlessly.

objects.title: Familiar
objects.text: For each selection, you can get a summon based on a mundane animal of your choosing. The summon is an autonomous construct made of aura. The animals' strength and speed are roughly relative to your own, with damage it takes depleting your aura.

objects.title: Fusion
objects.text: You can now merge your summons with each other and yourself. Either option adds together the physical capabilities of those merged. This does not grant you any summons on its own.

objects.title: True Self
objects.text: You can summon an autonomous, humanoid construct that is a representation of your own subconscious. It can use any other abilities of your semblance at will, regardless of how easily you can. Its range will all abilities is roughly 100m, regardless of your own. You can choose its initial appearance.

objects.title: Overclock
objects.text: When active, your physical and mental abilities are increased to double their ordinary maximum potential. If you have the aura, you can maintain this state indefinitely with no drawbacks.

objects.title: Ego Flight
objects.text: You can nullify the effect of gravity on and propel both yourself and objects you're touching. The propulsion scales off of the amount of aura used, but caps at roughly 80kmph (~50mph).

objects.title: Regalia
objects.text: A burst of energy erupts from your body and reforms over it as armor, the design can be consciously controlled, which is nearly indestructible and triples all of your physical abilities. The armor disappears when broken or when you stop supplying it with aura.

objects.title: Snatch
objects.text: You can create an invisible 'mark' on an object by touching it. You can passively sense marked objects in range and actively teleport them back into your hand, erasing the mark.

objects.title: Grimm Sense
objects.text: This is not the power to sense Grimm, but to instead to passively perceive the world as they do. What this means is that you can passively sense the negative emotions of those within range, serving as a radar of sorts.

objects.title: Clairvoyant
objects.text: You passively have a 360 degree field of view and can use aura to increase your vision to see through almost any substance in your semblance's range. This does not improve your vision.

objects.title: Nightmare
objects.text: You can make one person within range experience hallucinations based on their subconscious fears, making them functionally have a waking nightmare.

objects.title: Rabbit's Foot
objects.text: You and everyone in your range have a small, passive luck boost in non-combat situations. At will, you can exempt people from this effect.

objects.title: Mines
objects.text: You can create floating orbs within an arm's reach, which explode when someone else touches them or simply at will when they're in range. You're not immune to the explosions or their force.

objects.title: Clones
objects.text: You can create up to 2 clones that can fight alongside you. Their effective range is your current semblance range +10 meters. The clones are fairly durable, but they absorb hits using your aura and when destroyed or desummoned, their accumulated experiences, pain included, go to you.

objects.title: Grimm Shadow
objects.text: You can store Grimm in your shadow. The only real limit to how many you can fit is if you can even get them into your shadow at all, from which you can make chosen Grimm emerge at will. This does not let you control Grimm but does let you know exactly how many are stored. It also lets you enlarge your shadow.

objects.title: Monster Control
objects.text: You can control one of the Grimm within range at any given time, in addition to being able to sense the world through it.

objects.title: Grimm Manifestation
objects.text: By killing any Grimm, you can store a bit of their essence, which you can either use to create a hybridized Grimm or manifest the traits of Grimm over your own body, such as claws or wings. This does not grant a way to control the hybrid.

objects.title: False System
objects.text: When you close your eyes, you can see a heads-up display that visualizes your physical state as health, stamina, and aura bars, as well as displaying your skills and abilities, with your current level and progress to your next milestone being visualized as levels. This makes it so that your skills don't degrade, but does not help develop them further.

objects.title: Sleeper
objects.text: You can selectively induce drowsiness on those within range. Those with little to no aura can be put to sleep in moments but it takes considerably longer on those with as much or more aura than you.

objects.title: Path
objects.text: By focusing on a goal, you can figure out the best next step towards achieving it, as long as that step involves you. This does not guarantee being capable of what it needed or that you being involved is the best option.

objects.title: Hypnotic Gaze
objects.text: When making direct eye contact with someone, you can make them passive and suggestible. This can be used to make someone stop whatever they're currently doing and give simple commands, in addition to implanting ideas in their subconscious.

objects.title: Threadcount
objects.text: You can create razor sharp threads out of aura that you can freely control while in range. Using this effectively requires good multitasking capabilities. Threads become weaker the thinner they are, unless you pump more aura into them to counter this.

objects.title: Threat Analysis
objects.text: When looking at someone, you can see roughly how powerful they are relative to yourself, indicated by a reading from green to red, with green meaning that they aren't a threat to you at all and red meaning that you have nearly no chance of victory. This does not tell you what their abilities are.

objects.title: Semblance Detection
objects.text: By looking at someone, you can tell if they have an unlocked semblance and understand one word that broadly describes it.

objects.title: Acceleration
objects.text: You can increase the rate at which time flows for your body, increasing it to upwards of 3x the rate. This doesn't directly make you stronger, but speed is basically power, so you'll hit harder using this.

objects.title: Retaliation
objects.text: You can store kinetic energy up to the maximum amount your body can exert in a single, continuous movement, which you can redistribute and release from your body as you wish.

objects.title: Flashbang
objects.text: From your open palm, you can fire large, bright energy beams which temporarily blind those they hit. The beams deal no direct damage.

objects.title: Phantom
objects.text: You can enter a state which lets you, your clothing/equipment, and whatever you're holding become invisible. This doesn't make you unbeatable, but those who can't sense aura will struggle to do anything against you.

objects.title: Nobody Special
objects.text: You can enter a state wherein you can't be directly observed by someone's senses, requiring things like mirrors, cameras, sensory powers, or peripheral vision for someone to see you.

objects.title: Remote Copy
objects.text: You can use a copy of the semblance of a person within range, but only when actively focusing on them. This does not let you know what the semblance is or how it works, only how to activate, assuming its effect involves conscious activation. When you stop focusing on them, you lose the copy.

objects.title: Sealing
objects.text: By touching someone, you can leave an invisible mark on their body. When they're in range, you can sense a marked person and cut off their ability to consciously use their semblance. This does not impact passive semblance effects. The mark lasts for roughly 24 hours.

objects.title: Shifting
objects.text: You can partially shift your body and whatever you're holding or wearing into another dimension, letting things harmlessly pass through the space where it'd be. This can only shift mass equaling up to roughly 20% of your body's.

objects.title: Temperature Control
objects.text: You can alter the temperature of your body or a singular, continuous inanimate object within range that has less total mass than your body. The temperature alteration itself can range anywhere from -10°C to 100°C, with you being immune to the temperature changes created by your semblance.

objects.title: Glitch
objects.text: You can cause the senses of those within range to "misfire" in random ways. Maybe someone's mouth feels hot as if they ate a pepper, their vision could flicker, or the sounds they hear are distorted.

objects.title: Rewind
objects.text: You can "set" your current location, letting yourself teleport back to it ten seconds later. Each time, you can choose whether you retain your momentum or not. This also works on things you are wearing or holding.

objects.title: Domain
objects.text: You make a barrier over your entire range and impose one command on the people inside, with the option to exclude people from it if you wish. They will be compelled to follow this command but can choose to defy it if they have a strong will.

The vaunted ability to distort the shape, flow, and properties of Aura.

By distorting your own Aura, you can harden or soften its defenses, compress it into devastating strikes, reshape it into weapons or barriers, alter how it reinforces your body, or produce countless other effects limited only by your understanding and imagination.

This control extends to the Aura of others within a short range. You can disrupt an opponent's Aura, creating weaknesses, destabilizing their defenses, interfering with their Semblance, or forcing their Aura to behave in unnatural ways. Likewise, you may reshape an ally's Aura to better suit the situation, though manipulating another's Aura is significantly more difficult than your own.

The greater the distortion or the finer the control required, the more Aura, concentration, and practice it demands.
objects.title: Aura Distortion

addons.title: Aura Manipulation
addons.text:
Through the fusion of Distortion and Amplification, you now gain total mastery over your own Aura and those of others. You can even perceive, and comprehend the unique Aura flow of any Semblance you witness. You then restructure your own Aura to mirror that signature, allowing you to replicate the ability at will.

This mimicry is not limited to mere duplication. You can alter, refine, and change the internal parameters of the copied Semblance, allowing you to optimize its output to be more potent or efficient than the original.

Once you have learned a Semblance, you can resurface it indefinitely, provided you remember the understanding of its unique flow.

objects.text:
Your Semblance allows you to strengthen and stabilize Aura, both your own and others'. Aura Amplification also strengthens any Aura-based function.

Physical enhancement becomes greater, healing accelerates, Aura constructs grow denser and tougher, barriers become harder to break, and Aura manipulation becomes vastly more precise and efficient. Every application of Aura performs at a higher level while wasting less energy.

Semblances benefit just as greatly. By amplifying the Aura that fuels them, you can increase a Semblance's power, range, duration, stability, or efficiency depending on its nature. With sufficient control, you can even overclock a Semblance beyond its normal limits for short bursts, achieving feats otherwise impossible.

Your power can be projected to multiple allies simultaneously. A skilled user can transform an entire team into stronger, faster, tougher, and more capable versions of themselves.

The only true limitation is your own Aura. Stronger amplifications and additional targets increase Aura consumption exponentially.
objects.title: Aura Amp

objects.text:
This Semblance summons around the Caster a series of Runic arrays and Spell matrixes that can be arranged into a wide variety of effects, so long as you understand how to build them. At first, you instinctively know only a few basic forms: reinforcement, alteration, structural grasp, arcane bullets, simple barriers, and other standard patterns that can be expressed through Aura and Dust.

The range, speed, and complexity of what you can produce depend entirely on your knowledge, control, and reserves. More advanced effects must be designed, tested, and refined before they can be used reliably, and attempting to shape a matrix you do not fully understand can cause anything from a failed activation to a dangerous backlash.

Your intuition for arcane structure is also unusually sharp, letting you rapidly grasp foundational patterns and pre-set techniques you have already learned, which greatly improves the speed at which you can deploy them again.
objects.title: Arcane Circle

objects.text:
You can slowly, permanently invest a portion of your aura into a single object — your weapon, a piece of armor, a ring, or whatever else in your possession.

Initially, this manifests strictly as enhancements to the item's properties. A sword becomes sharper and harder to chip; a shield gains the integrity to absorb and disperse more force than it should normally can; and a suit of armor is reshaped to be not only tougher than titanium, but entirely lightweight, flexible, and comfortable to wear.

The only catch is that any Aura poured into an object is locked away, reducing your maximum active reserves until you can sleep.

Only after a certain amount of Aura has been invested into an item, would it then start allow you to guide its effect into Enchantment. These Enchantments growth are based on the time and Aura dedicated to it:

  • After 10 Days: The object begins to manifest weak, supernatural effects. A blade might develop an ethereal edge that go through physical armor, or a shield might generate a barrier of energy to deflect projectiles.

  • After 20 Days: The item begins to harbor a spark of the soul, granting it utility comparable to a weaker Semblance.

  • After 1 Month: The enchantment effect grows into a fully-realized Semblance-level capability. A ring could grant its wearer hyper-accelerated cognitive function and super-learning; Glasses could visually predict the future seconds or minutes in advance; and a sword could create, shape, and bend an element to your whim.

Given enough time, patience, and a vast collection of items, you could become a global threat while dressed in nothing but enchanted pajamas.

There is no known ceiling to this growth.
objects.title: Aura Enchantment

objects.text: You can break your body down into a cloud of rose-petal-like motes and reassemble elsewhere within a short range, moving at extreme speed in between. You can't attack or interact with the world while dispersed, but you can use it to close distance, dodge, or reposition behind an enemy. Extended use leaves you dizzy; not a great idea in long fights.
objects.title: Petal Burst

objects.text: You can push and pull on ferrous metals within your range — enough to redirect projectiles, yank weapons out of grips, or pull yourself toward a steel beam. Subtle applications are easier to get away with than flashy ones; with practice, you can make a foe's sword veer an inch off-target without anyone noticing. Doesn't work on non-ferrous metals.
objects.title: Polarity

objects.text: You can absorb electricity into your body with no harm, storing it to briefly supercharge your physical attributes. The boost is explosive but short-lived, and the more charge you hold, the harder it is to control. Lightning dust, broken power lines, and certain Grimm all make excellent fuel. Don't try to store more than your body can hold.
objects.title: High Voltage

objects.text: A passive aura of bad luck radiates from you, subtly affecting everyone nearby — enemies and allies alike. Shots miss by an inch, footing gives out, machines jam. You have some conscious control over who it affects, but not perfect control. This is a heavier burden than it sounds; you'll want to spend time alone.
objects.title: Misfortune

objects.text: A rare inversion of the misfortune-type semblance. A passive aura of good luck radiates from you, subtly improving outcomes for everyone nearby — allies and enemies alike. Shots connect by an inch, footing holds, machines cooperate. Like misfortune, you can tilt it but not fully control it. Unlike misfortune, you'll rarely want to be alone.
objects.title: Fortune's Smile

objects.text: You can project convincing visual and auditory illusions into the minds of those within your range. The illusions are perfect to the viewer but don't physically exist — cameras don't pick them up, and touching one reveals nothing's there. Each target requires concentration; more than three at once is a strain.
objects.title: Hallucinations

objects.text: Damage you take is partially absorbed and stored as kinetic energy, which you can then channel into your next strikes. The bigger the hit you took, the harder you hit back. This doesn't make you tougher — you still take the damage — but it gives a means of paying it back with interest. Don't get greedy; there's a cap.
objects.title: Burn

objects.text: You can render yourself completely invisible to the naked eye and most sensors for as long as you hold your aura steady. Attacking, taking damage, or moving too quickly breaks the effect. Sound, smell, and aura-sense still reveal you. Pair with stealth training for best results.
objects.title: Camouflage

objects.text: By touching someone, you can ask a yes-or-no question and receive an honest answer — not by reading their mind, but by a flash of intuition that tells you whether what they're saying is true. The question has to be something the target actually knows the answer to; opinions and predictions just return static.
objects.title: Foresight

objects.text: You can mask the negative emotions of yourself and those nearby, rendering you effectively invisible to Grimm — who track by negative emotion. This doesn't make the emotions go away; it just smooths them out where Grimm can feel them. Extended use leaves you emotionally numb for hours afterward.
objects.title: Tranquility

objects.text: Channel stored energy into your weapon for a single, devastating strike — the more you've absorbed, the harder the hit. Unlike Burn, this requires you to sheathe your weapon between absorbing and striking, telegraphing the attack to anyone paying attention. The payoff, when it lands, is considerable.
objects.title: Moonslice

objects.text: You can step into a shadow and emerge from another shadow within line of sight, leaving no trace in between. The shadows have to be large enough to fit your body, and the destination has to be one you can currently see. You can bring passengers, but the Aura cost rises exponentially.
objects.title: Shadow Step
Spark of Ingenuity
objects.text:
You have become an Omni-Tinker, capable of drawing upon every field of technology rather than a single specialty.

Initially, your insight extends only slightly beyond Remnant's current level of scientific understanding, producing innovations that appear as natural, if extraordinary, advancements of existing technology. However every device you construct broadens your understanding of the principles behind it, steadily unlocking increasingly advanced concepts across the technological disciplines involved.

Your intuition also eagerly assimilates new information. Careful study of unfamiliar machinery, Dust applications, Aura, Semblances, Grimm, or other anomalous phenomena can reveal entirely new avenues of development, granting partial access to technological branches derived from what you have observed.

Continued experimentation and analysis deepen that understanding, eventually allowing you to reproduce, emulate, or engineer devices based upon even the most exotic abilities or phenomena.

Your imagination is effectively limitless; your resources are not.

addons.title: Blasphemous Eyes
addons.text:
When the Sharingan and Rinnegan fully resonate, they ascend into the primordial Rinne Sharingan—the origin from which all ocular powers descend.

The strengths of both dōjutsu cease functioning as separate abilities and instead become a seamless whole. Your perception transcends conventional space and time, allowing you to effortlessly observe, analyze, predict, and manipulate phenomena beyond the reach of either eye alone.

Your illusions evolve into divine techniques capable of affecting entire battlefields at once, trapping countless minds within a shared reality or weaving false worlds indistinguishable from truth itself.

Space bends naturally before your gaze. Portals, dimensional travel, and reality-warping techniques become extensions of instinct rather than effort.

The Susanoo and Six Paths merge into a single transcendent expression of your power, while your command over Aura, souls, gravity, and illusion approaches mythic proportions.

Few beings in existence could hope to oppose one who has awakened the Rinne Sharingan.

objects.title: Inherited Gaze
objects.text:
Your eyes carry an ancient dormant power of the Sharingan, an ocular Dojustu that awakens through intense emotional turmoil. Each stage is permanent once reached, though its evolution is entirely beyond your control.

As the Sharingan matures, your vision grows unnaturally acute, allowing you to perceive the flow of combat, read an opponent's movements before they happen, and instinctively understand techniques witnessed through your own eyes. Mundane combat styles, weapon techniques, and Aura manipulation can be faithfully reproduced, provided your body is capable of performing them.

The Sharingan is also an Eye of Hypnosis. Through eye contact, you can cast increasingly powerful illusions that deceive the senses, manipulate perception, and ensnare the mind. At its highest awakening, the Mangekyō Sharingan grants a unique ocular ability in each eye, as well as the power to manifest the legendary Susanoo.

objects.title: The Eyes of God
objects.text:
The legendary Rinnegan represents the final evolution of ocular power, standing above all other dōjutsu.

Your perception expands beyond the physical world itself, allowing you to perceive Aura, souls, invisible forces, dimensional distortions, and the fundamental flow of energy with perfect clarity.

The Rinnegan grants mastery over six transcendent Paths, each embodying a different dominion.

  • Deva Path: Control attractive and repulsive forces, manipulate gravity, and unleash devastating omnidirectional shockwaves.

  • Asura Path: Transform your body into living weaponry through Aura constructs and mechanical augmentation.

  • Human Path: Perceive, extract, or directly interact with souls through physical contact.

  • Animal Path: Summon powerful creatures bound by your Aura.

  • Preta Path: Absorb Aura, Dust, and other supernatural energies directed against you.

  • Naraka Path: Summon the King of Hell to interrogate, judge, restore, or repair those brought before it.

Beyond the Six Paths, the Rinnegan grants command over black receivers forged from condensed Aura, mastery of sealing techniques, the ability to create remote bodies linked by shared vision, and limited manipulation of life and death itself.

The Rinnegan exists independently of the Sharingan, though the powers of both may be wielded simultaneously should you possess them.

objects.title: Diminishing Distance
objects.text: You project an unseen boundary around yourself within which anything approaching slows the closer it gets, converging toward you but never quite arriving unless you allow it. At full strength, almost nothing can close the distance to touch you. Maintaining it demands total stillness and constant focus, and the instant you move to touch or attack something yourself, the boundary collapses and needs time to reset.

objects.title: Hollow Reserve
objects.text: Where Aura draws on a soul's own light, yours draws on the opposite. You can pull in the fear, grief, hatred, and despair radiating from yourself and everyone nearby, converting it into a corrosive reserve of power that fuels you. It never runs dry as long as misery is close at hand, but it comes with real costs: Grimm are drawn to you like a lighthouse.

objects.title: Twisted Reflection
